---
title: "01_data_preprocessing"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(vroom)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION: INPUT DATA PATHS
# ==============================================================================
# Please specify the file paths to your input abundance tables here.
# If you are reproducing this analysis, update these paths to match your local environment.

# 1. Path to the Illumina (MiSeq) abundance table (TSV format)
#    Expected format: QIIME2 output or similar feature table
illumina_input_path <- "./miseq/table_species_V3-V4_relative/table_species_V3-V4_relative.tsv"

# 2. Path to the Nanopore (ONT) abundance table (TSV format)
#    Expected format: EPI2ME wf-16s output
nanopore_input_path <- "./epi2melabs/instances/wf-16s_01K5Z7KHWZP8KBZPWVWT3QSX91/output/abundance_table_species.tsv"

# ==============================================================================
```

```{r}
# --- 1.1 Process Illumina (miseq) Data (Step-by-Step) ---

# Define sample mapping and taxonomy levels
illumina_samples <- c(AP = "1749", BP = "1750", AS = "1751", BS = "1752")
tax_levels <- c("kingdom", "phylum", "class", "order", "family", "genus", "species")

# --- Step 1.1.1: Import, select, and rename columns ---
# First, read the file using the path defined in the configuration chunk.
print(paste("Loading Illumina data from:", illumina_input_path))

illumina_renamed <- vroom::vroom(
  file = illumina_input_path,
  delim = "\t", 
  skip = 1,
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  dplyr::rename(taxonomy = 1) %>%
  dplyr::select(taxonomy, !!!illumina_samples)

print("--- Step 1.1: After renaming and selecting ---")
head(illumina_renamed)

# --- Step 1.1.2: Convert to rounded percentages ---
# Now, convert the abundance data to the desired format.
illumina_rel_abundance <- illumina_renamed %>%
  dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(
    .cols = where(is.numeric),
    .fns = ~ round(.x * 100, 3)
  ))

print("--- Step 1.1.2: After converting to percentages ---")
head(illumina_rel_abundance)

# --- Step 1.1.3: Separate and clean taxonomy ---
# Finally, perform all the taxonomy manipulations.
illumina_processed <- illumina_rel_abundance %>%
  tidyr::separate(
    col = taxonomy,
    into = tax_levels,
    sep = ";",
    fill = "right"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(
      .cols = dplyr::all_of(tax_levels), 
      .fns = ~ stringr::str_remove(., "^[kpcofgsd]?__")
    ),
    dplyr::across(
      .cols = dplyr::all_of(tax_levels), 
      .fns = ~ dplyr::na_if(., "")
    )
  )

print("--- Step 1.1.3: Final cleaned Illumina data ---")
head(illumina_processed)
```

```{r}
# --- 1.2 Process Nanopore (ONT) Data (Step-by-Step) ---

# Define sample mapping and taxonomy levels
nanopore_samples <- c(AP = "barcode07", BP = "barcode08", AS = "barcode09", BS = "barcode10")
temp_tax_levels <- c("kingdom", "sub_kingdom", "phylum", "class", "order", "family", "genus", "species")
final_tax_levels <- c("kingdom", "phylum", "class", "order", "family", "genus", "species")


# --- Step 1.2.1: Import, select, and rename columns ---
print(paste("Loading Nanopore data from:", nanopore_input_path))

nanopore_renamed <- vroom::vroom(
  file = nanopore_input_path,
  delim = "\t",
  show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::rename(taxonomy = 1) %>%
  dplyr::select(taxonomy, !!!nanopore_samples)

print("--- Step 1.2.1: After renaming and selecting ---")
head(nanopore_renamed)


# --- Step 1.2.2: Convert counts to rounded percentages ---
nanopore_rel_abundance <- nanopore_renamed %>%
  dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(
    .cols = where(is.numeric),
    .fns = ~ round(.x / sum(.x) * 100, 3)
  ))

print("--- Step 1.2.2: After converting to percentages ---")
head(nanopore_rel_abundance)

# --- Step 1.2.3: Separate and clean taxonomy ---
nanopore_processed <- nanopore_rel_abundance %>%
  tidyr::separate(col = taxonomy, into = temp_tax_levels, sep = ";", fill = "right") %>%
  # Skip sub_kingdom
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(final_tax_levels), dplyr::all_of(names(nanopore_samples))) %>%
  # Handle "Unclassified" and "Unknown"
  dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(
    .cols = dplyr::all_of(final_tax_levels),
    .fns = ~ dplyr::if_else(tolower(.x) %in% c("unclassified", "unknown"), NA_character_, .x)
  )) %>%
  # Clean species column (remove duplicated genus)
  dplyr::mutate(
    species = dplyr::if_else(
      !is.na(genus) & stringr::str_starts(species, paste0(genus, " ")),
      stringr::str_remove(species, paste0("^", genus, " ")),
      species
    )
  ) %>%
  # Handle species like "Unclassified_Lachnoanaerobaculum"
  dplyr::mutate(
    species = dplyr::if_else(
      stringr::str_starts(species, "Unclassified"),
      NA_character_,
      species
    )
  ) %>% 

  # --- Final step: Standardize placeholder taxon names ---
  dplyr::mutate(
    species = species %>%
      # replace "oral taxon ..." pattern
      stringr::str_replace("oral taxon (\\d+)", "HMT-\\1")
  )

print("--- Step 2.3: Final cleaned Nanopore data ---")
head(nanopore_processed)
```
```{r}
# --- 1.3 Manual Taxonomic Harmonization ---
# This step corrects taxonomic inconsistencies between the two pipelines
# (e.g., due to QIIME2 using HOMD taxonomy vs. wf-16s using NCBI taxonomy).

# 1. Define the correction map
# Here, we define the rules for harmonization.
tax_correction_map <- tibble::tribble(
  ~condition_column, ~condition_value, ~target_column, ~correct_value,
  # Rule 1: If genus is 'Campylobacter', ensure its phylum is 'Campylobacterota'
  "genus", "Campylobacter", "phylum", "Campylobacterota",
  
  # Rule 2: (Example) If genus is 'Porphyromonas', ensure its family is 'Porphyromonadaceae'
  # "genus", "Porphyromonas", "family", "Porphyromonadaceae",
  
  # ... Add more rules here as you find them ...
  # "species", "fur", "species", "fur (HMT-488)",
)

print("--- Applying manual taxonomic corrections ---")
print(tax_correction_map)

# 2. Apply the corrections to both datasets using a loop
for(i in 1:nrow(tax_correction_map)) {
  rule <- tax_correction_map[i, ]
  
  # Apply correction to Illumina data
  illumina_processed <- illumina_processed %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      # The !!sym() syntax allows us to use the text from our map as column names
      !!rule$target_column := dplyr::if_else(
        # Condition: If the condition column contains the specified value...
        !is.na(!!rlang::sym(rule$condition_column)) & !!rlang::sym(rule$condition_column) == rule$condition_value,
        # If TRUE: Apply the correction
        rule$correct_value,
        # If FALSE: Keep the original value
        !!rlang::sym(rule$target_column)
      )
    )
  
  # Apply the same correction to Nanopore data
  nanopore_processed <- nanopore_processed %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      !!rule$target_column := dplyr::if_else(
        !is.na(!!rlang::sym(rule$condition_column)) & !!rlang::sym(rule$condition_column) == rule$condition_value,
        rule$correct_value,
        !!rlang::sym(rule$target_column)
      )
    )
}

# --- Verify one of the changes ---
print("--- Verification of 'Campylobacter' correction in Illumina data ---")
illumina_processed %>% 
  dplyr::filter(genus == "Campylobacter") %>%
  dplyr::select(phylum, genus) %>%
  head()
```

```{r}
# --- 1.4 Advanced Taxonomic Harmonization (Full Lineage Correction) ---

# 1. Define the full lineage correction map
# Here, we define rules to replace an entire taxonomic line.
# The key to identify the row to be changed is the original, problematic 'species' name.
lineage_correction_map <- tibble::tribble(
  ~species_original,         ~phylum_new,     ~class_new,        ~order_new,          ~family_new,        ~genus_new,      ~species_new,
  # --- Rules for TM7/Saccharibacteria taxa ---
  "TM7 phylum sp. HMT-352", "Patescibacteria", "Saccharimonadia", "Saccharimonadales", "Nanosynbacteraceae", "Nanosynbacter", "sp. HMT-352",
  "TM7 phylum sp. HMT-346", "Patescibacteria", "Saccharimonadia", "Saccharimonadales", "Saccharimonadaceae", "Saccharimonas", "sp. HMT-346",
  "TM7 phylum sp. HMT-347", "Patescibacteria", "Saccharimonadia", "Saccharimonadales", "Saccharimonadaceae", "Saccharimonas", "sp. HMT-347",
  "TM7 phylum sp. HMT-349", "Patescibacteria", "Saccharimonadia", "Saccharimonadales", "Saccharimonadaceae", "Saccharimonas", "sp. HMT-349",
  "TM7 phylum sp. HMT-351", "Patescibacteria", "Saccharimonadia", "Saccharimonadales", "Nanosyncoccaceae", "Nanosyncoccus", "sp. HMT-351",
   "TM7 phylum sp. HMT-348", "Patescibacteria", "Saccharimonadia", "Saccharimonadales", "Parvisynbacteraceae", "Parvisynbacter", "sp. HMT-348",
   "TM7 bacterium human oral taxon HOT-869", "Patescibacteria", "Saccharimonadia", "Saccharimonadales", "Saccharimonadaceae", "Saccharimonas", "sp. HMT-869",
  
  # below were reffered by searching qiime.taxonomy etc.  
  # "Candidatus Saccharibacteria bacterium HMT-488", "Patescibacteria", "Saccharimonadia", "Saccharimonadales", "Nanosynbacteraceae", "Nanosynbacter", "fur (HMT-488)",
"TM7 phylum sp. HMT-356", "Patescibacteria", "Saccharimonadia", "Saccharimonadales", "Nanoperiomorbaceae", "Nanoperiomorbus", "periodonticus",
)

print("--- Applying full lineage taxonomic corrections ---")

# 2. Apply the corrections to the Nanopore data using a join
nanopore_processed <- nanopore_processed %>%
  # Join the correction map to the main data.
  # The join key is the original species name.
  dplyr::left_join(lineage_correction_map, by = c("species" = "species_original")) %>%
  
  # Update the original columns using the new "_new" columns.
  # coalesce() is perfect for this: it takes the first non-NA value.
  # If a correction exists (e.g., phylum_new is not NA), its value is used.
  # Otherwise (if phylum_new is NA), the original `phylum` value is kept.
  dplyr::mutate(
    phylum  = coalesce(phylum_new, phylum),
    class   = coalesce(class_new, class),
    order   = coalesce(order_new, order),
    family  = coalesce(family_new, family),
    genus   = coalesce(genus_new, genus),
    species = coalesce(species_new, species)
  ) %>%
  
  # Remove the temporary "_new" columns, which are no longer needed
  dplyr::select(-ends_with("_new"))

# (Optional) Apply the same logic to Illumina data if needed, to ensure consistency
# illumina_processed <- illumina_processed %>%

# --- Verify one of the changes ---
print("--- Verification of TM7 lineage correction in Nanopore data ---")
nanopore_processed %>% 
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(species, "HMT-352")) %>%
  dplyr::select(phylum, class, order, family, genus, species)
```

```{r}
# --- 1.5 Save Processed Data Objects ---

# Create a directory to store processed data if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("processed_data")) {
  dir.create("processed_data")
}

# Save the final, cleaned data frames as RDS files
saveRDS(illumina_processed, file = "processed_data/illumina_processed.rds")
saveRDS(nanopore_processed, file = "processed_data/nanopore_processed.rds")

print("Processed data files have been saved to the 'processed_data' folder.")
```
